comments: The sensor noise floor across this image is essentially nonexistent — when we run the Laplacian residual across all three channels, the variance sits at roughly 0.003, which just doesn't happen with real camera hardware. Even a clean studio shot on a Sony A7 will leave measurable photon noise in the shadows. On top of that, the GAN fingerprint sweep turned up 41 periodic spikes in the FFT magnitude — these are the kind of evenly-spaced spectral artifacts that diffusion models leave behind in the high-frequency bands. The neon lighting looks convincing at first glance, but the cross-channel color correlation is 0.96, meaning the RGB channels are moving almost identically — real lighting never does that. The face geometry also shows bilateral symmetry at 0.93, which is well above what you'd expect from a real person. Skin texture plasticity index is 0.87 — basically the whole face renders as one smooth synthetic surface.
metadata: No EXIF data present anywhere in the file. No camera make, model, focal length, or GPS coordinates. The image is 1456x816 pixels — a non-standard widescreen ratio strongly associated with Midjourney widescreen prompt output. No lens profile, no ICC color space matching any physical capture device.
spectral: GAN fingerprint confirmed with 41 periodic spikes detected in the 2D FFT magnitude spectrum (score 3.74). Radial power spectral density slope measured at -1.09, far outside the -2.2 to -2.9 range we expect from real photography. Wavelet detail energy in the diagonal sub-band came back at 0.11 — heavily suppressed, consistent with a diffusion model smoothing out the fine detail layer.
